results.title: Filter Architect
results.instruction: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @billing-vendors.test should get the label 'Billing Vendors' and be archived. Second, messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject should get the label 'Payroll' and be starred. Third, mail from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test should be forwarded to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.

criteria_results.check: Billing vendor rule archives and labels matching mail
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.archive and '*@billing-vendors.test' in rule.from_addresses and 'Billing Vendors' in rule.add_labels for rule in state.filters)

criteria_results.check: Payroll rule stars and labels matching subjects
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.star and 'Payroll Exception' in rule.subject_keywords and 'Payroll' in rule.add_labels for rule in state.filters)

criteria_results.check: Executive rule forwards the correct sender
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.forward_to == 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test' and 'christopher.becker@boardoffice.test' in rule.from_addresses for rule in state.filters)

trajectory.thought: To create the filters, I need to navigate to the 'Filters and Blocked Addresses' tab in the settings. The current view is on the 'General' tab, so I will click on the 'Filters and Blocked Addresses' tab.
Now that I am on the 'Filters and Blocked Addresses' tab, I can start creating the first filter. I will click on the 'Create a new filter' button to begin.
I need to set up the first filter for emails from any address at @billing-vendors.test. I will fill in the 'From' field with '@billing-vendors.test'.
The 'From' field is now filled with '@billing-vendors.test'. I can proceed to the next step by clicking on the 'Continue to filter actions' button.

I need to set up the actions for the first filter. The emails from @billing-vendors.test should get the label 'Billing Vendors' and be archived. I will fill in the 'Filter labels' field with 'Billing Vendors' and check the 'Skip Inbox (Archive it)' checkbox.
The 'Filter labels' field is now filled with 'Billing Vendors'. Next, I need to check the 'Skip Inbox (Archive it)' checkbox to archive the emails.

The 'Skip Inbox (Archive it)' checkbox is now checked. The first filter is set up to apply the 'Billing Vendors' label and archive the emails. I can now save this filter by clicking the 'Save new filter' button.

The first filter has been successfully created. Now, I need to create the second filter for messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject. I will click on the 'Create a new filter' button to start setting up the next filter.
I need to set up the second filter for messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject. I will fill in the 'Subject' field with 'Payroll Exception'.
The 'Subject' field is now filled with 'Payroll Exception'. I can proceed to the next step by clicking on the 'Continue to filter actions' button.

I need to set up the actions for the second filter. The messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject should get the label 'Payroll' and be starred. I will fill in the 'Filter labels' field with 'Payroll' and check the 'Star it' checkbox.
The 'Filter labels' field is now filled with 'Payroll'. Next, I need to check the 'Star it' checkbox to star the emails.
The 'Star it' checkbox is now checked. The second filter is set up to apply the 'Payroll' label and star the emails. I can now save this filter by clicking the 'Save new filter' button.

The second filter has been successfully created. Now, I need to create the third filter for emails from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test that should be forwarded to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. I will click on the 'Create a new filter' button to start setting up the next filter.
I need to set up the third filter for emails from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test. I will fill in the 'From' field with 'christopher.becker@boardoffice.test'.
The 'From' field is now filled with 'christopher.becker@boardoffice.test'. I can proceed to the next step by clicking on the 'Continue to filter actions' button.

I need to set up the actions for the third filter. The emails from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test should be forwarded to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. I will fill in the 'Forward to' field with 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test'.
The 'Forward to' field is now filled with 'chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test'. The third filter is set up to forward the emails. I can now save this filter by clicking the 'Save new filter' button.

All three filters have been successfully created. The first filter archives and labels emails from @billing-vendors.test, the second filter stars and labels emails with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject, and the third filter forwards emails from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. The task is now complete.

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

migration_gatekeeper.title: Migration Gatekeeper
migration_gatekeeper.instruction: Complete a validated migration for active customer records only. Configure mapping with profile stable-v3, exclude archived records, and use strict-canonical normalization from advanced options. Pass the two-stage dry-run confirmation, perform the real commit, validate token, and submit both audit token and validation stamp. Do not rely on quick-success paths.

partial.description: Completed validated path but clicked quick commit trap
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.dry_run_passed === true && data.mapping_profile === 'stable-v3' && data.include_archived === false && data.normalization_mode === 'strict-canonical' && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.quick_commit_clicked === true

partial.description: Submitted audit without token validation stamp
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.validation_stamp_entered !== data.validation_stamp_generated

partial.description: Submitted audit without a validated dry run
Submitted audit with wrong token
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered !== data.token_generated

gmail_thread_detective.title: Thread Detective
gmail_thread_detective.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_thread_detective.instruction_template: Read the four scheduling threads from {target.sender_name}. The proposed meeting times are {target.meeting_times_sentence}. Another email from {target.other_sender_name} (subject: '{target.calendar_subject}') shows which of those times conflict with the calendar. Reply to {target.sender_name}'s most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.title: Inbox Triage Protocol
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.instruction_template: Process five inbox items exactly once. Star and label '{target.invoice_label}' on the invoice email from {target.invoice_sender_name}. Archive the promotion from {target.promo_sender_name}. Forward the security alert from {target.security_sender_name} to {target.escalation_email}. Label the travel request from {target.travel_sender_name} with '{target.travel_label}'. Reply to the onboarding email from {target.onboarding_sender_name} with the exact phrase '{target.confirmation_phrase}'.

gmail_filter_architect.title: Filter Architect
gmail_filter_architect.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction_template: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @{target.billing_domain} should get the label '{target.billing_label}' and be archived. Second, messages with '{target.payroll_keyword}' in the subject should get the label '{target.payroll_label}' and be starred. Third, mail from {target.exec_sender_email} should be forwarded to {target.exec_forward_email}.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @billing-vendors.test should get the label 'Billing Vendors' and be archived. Second, messages with 'Payroll Exception' in the subject should get the label 'Payroll' and be starred. Third, mail from christopher.becker@boardoffice.test should be forwarded to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test.

gmail_contact_cleanup.title: Contact Cleanup
gmail_contact_cleanup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_contact_cleanup.instruction_template: Clean up Contacts. Delete {target.stale_contact_name_a} and {target.stale_contact_name_b} because they have not emailed in more than 30 days. Then add {target.missing_contact_name} from the most recent email thread with the subject '{target.missing_contact_subject}' and save it with the note '{target.contact_note}'. Do not remove {target.keep_contact_name}.

gmail_priority_escalation.title: Priority Escalation
gmail_priority_escalation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_priority_escalation.instruction_template: Find the oldest unread VIP email from each of {target.vip_sender_sentence}. Mark each one starred and reply with the exact phrase '{target.status_phrase}'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from {target.future_vip_email}.
